risk.reason: Package-facing uncategorized skill included live MCP server probing, SSRF target testing, unauthenticated endpoint checks, and tool-description prompt-injection handling. It required review/redaction before routine metadata classification, manual discovery expansion, catalog endorsement, package endorsement, or publication endorsement.

risk.reason: OpenClaw has MIT license and high usefulness, but source review found high-risk real messaging/account/channel integrations, browser/canvas/nodes/cron tools, gateway exposure, host tool access, daemon/onboarding flow, and sandbox policy surfaces. risk.action: Closed after defining a conditional safe boundary: OpenClaw may only inform generic original runtime-surface safety review patterns. risk.reason: Metadata backfill directly reviewed Browser Trace and found browser/CDP session attachment, screenshots, DOM dumps, Browserbase API key mention, local debugger command snippets, and raw network/console/runtime filesystem traces. This needed high-risk review before metadata/catalog endorsement.
risk.action: Closed after converting Browser Trace to a high-risk, requires_review, authorized-read-only defensive debugging wrapper; local debugger launch, trace capture/query, Browserbase/API-key, and browser-session operational snippets were removed from package-facing guidance.
